page: Amazon Web Services r5d.16xlarge is a memory-optimized instance featuring 64 vCPUs and 32 physical cores powered by an Intel Xeon 8259CL processor running at 3.1 GHz. The system includes 512.0 GB of DDR4 memory operating at 3 GHz, providing a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per core. It is built on the Nitro hypervisor and utilizes the x86_64 architecture.

This instance type includes 2400 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, offering high-speed local data access directly bundled with the compute resources. Networking capabilities feature a baseline bandwidth of 20 Gbps, facilitating rapid data transfer across cloud environments.

Benchmark results indicate strong memory performance, particularly in large-block read-only and write-only operations, which register at 209.41 GB/sec and 92.36 GB/sec respectively. However, single-core processing performance is relatively weak, as shown by stress-ng and Geekbench single-core scores. Multi-core CPU performance and database operations, such as Redis SET operations and Passmark database tests, fall within the average tier.

Given the high memory density and integrated NVMe storage, this instance offers qualitative cost efficiency for workloads requiring large memory footprints alongside high-speed local scratch space. It is well-suited for high-performance databases, in-memory caches, big data analytics, and memory-intensive enterprise applications.
description: Amazon Web Services r5d.16xlarge is a memory-optimized instance designed for memory-intensive workloads requiring high-speed local storage. Powered by a 3.1 GHz Intel Xeon 8259CL processor, it features 64 vCPUs, 32 physical cores, and 512.0 GB of DDR4 memory. The instance includes 2400 GB of local NVMe SSD storage and a baseline network bandwidth of 20 Gbps. Benchmark data shows strong memory bandwidth performance for large block operations, reaching 209.41 GB/sec for reads and 92.36 GB/sec for writes, while single-core CPU performance remains in the lower tier. Multi-core processing and database operations perform at average levels. This hardware profile makes the r5d.16xlarge highly suitable for large-scale databases, in-memory caching, and data analytics workloads that benefit from a high memory-to-vCPU ratio and fast local storage.
og_description: Amazon Web Services r5d.16xlarge is a memory-optimized instance with 64 vCPUs, 512 GB DDR4 RAM, and 2400 GB local NVMe SSD storage, designed for databases and memory-intensive workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services r5d.16xlarge is a memory-optimized instance featuring 64 vCPUs, 512 GB RAM, and 2400 GB of local NVMe SSD storage.
tagline: A memory-optimized instance featuring high-capacity DDR4 RAM and integrated NVMe SSD storage for demanding database and analytical workloads.
bullet_points: 64 vCPUs and 512 GB of DDR4 memory
2400 GB of high-speed local NVMe SSD storage
Intel Xeon 8259CL processor running at 3.1 GHz
Strong large-block memory read and write bandwidth
Optimized for databases, in-memory caches, and data analytics
